content: Most existing explorations frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective sequential improvement framework, but we can repurpose its core no-training self-feedback mechanism to build a multi-perspective LLM self-reflection system that leverages inconsistent output iterations to expose blind spots a single perspective would miss. This creates a lightweight self-reflection structure that requires no extra training, supervised data, or external tools, addressing a key limitation of most multi-perspective reflection methods that rely on fine-tuning or auxiliary models.
